name: Ropa Vieja
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup lard, bacon drippings, or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the flank steak in half horizontally so it will fit into a large Dutch oven. Place it in the pot and cover it with water. Add the six whole cloves of garlic, along with the peppercorns and salt to taste. Place over low heat and bring the liquid to a simmer. Cover and continue cooking over low heat, turning the meat occasionally, until it is tender and well done, about one and a half to two hours.
2. While the meat is stewing, roast, peel, and seed the peppers. Slice each pepper lengthwise into strips that are 1/8 inch wide. Set aside.
3. When the meat is tender, remove the Dutch oven from the heat and let it cool on the counter. Once it's cool enough to handle, remove the meat from the broth and place it on a cutting board. Slice the meat across the grain into strips about two inches long. Using your fingers, pull the meat into fine shreds. Return it to the broth.
4. Heat the lard or other fat in a large heavy skillet over medium heat until it is rippling. Crush the sliced garlic cloves and add to the hot fat. Sauté for one minute, stirring frequently. Add the sliced onions and sauté until they are soft, about three minutes. Add the reserved pepper strips to the pan and continue sautéing and stirring for about two more minutes.
5. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the peppers, onion, and garlic to the Dutch oven. Cook the meat mixture, uncovered, over medium heat, stirring frequently until the flavors are well blended, about 10 to 15 minutes. Serve the meat hot, rolled up in heated flour tortillas.
